title: Submit V18.2: 608-dim Reversible V8 Engine
author: mini-sarami
body:
This approach maximizes the model's representational capacity within the strict 16MB (int8+zlib) limit through structural compression and an entropy-aware sizing strategy.

1. Cross-Layer Weight Tying:
To maximize performance within the size limit, we implemented a mirrored network structure where the first and second halves of the transformer share identical weights. This maintains a logical depth of 10 layers while effectively halving the physical parameter footprint.

2. Expanding Hidden Dimension to 608:
Leveraging the spatial margin created by weight tying, we significantly expanded the model_dim to 608. This drastically increases information throughput and representational capacity, maximizing learning efficiency for complex patterns.

3. Parameter-Free Learning Acceleration:
To accelerate training without adding file size, we integrated the following mechanisms:

Bigram Hash Embedding: Hashes bigrams into token embeddings to accelerate early-stage context comprehension.

Cross-Step Attention: Blends current and previous step inputs to smooth gradient propagation and reach deeper loss minimums.

Squared LeakyReLU: A computationally lightweight yet highly expressive activation that maximizes the number of steps executed within the 10-minute limit.

4. Entropy-Aware Compression Strategy:
As model weights become more complex during training (entropy increase), zlib compressibility inevitably degrades. We pre-calculated this entropy-driven "size bloat" (approx. 0.5MB over 13,000 steps). By precisely tuning our initial compressed size to ~15.4MB, our final submission optimally lands just below the absolute 16.0MB hard limit.
